title: Process Signals, Abort Trees, Listener Cleanup, and Graceful Shutdown
description: Every request owns an unrelated controller, SIGINT calls process.exit immediately, event listeners accumulate, pending readline and fetch work survive close, and aborted work prints as a defect. Learners cumulatively produce composed abort-aware Node session lifecycle through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Translate approved process signals into one root AbortController in the executable shell and define first and repeated signal behavior.
Compose session, command, timeout, and waiter signals while preserving reason and avoiding controller ownership confusion.
Use once or signal-bound listeners, remove handlers on completion, and prove no listener-count growth across commands.
